title: UDLM Common Elements — canonical shared $defs
description: Machine-referenceable canonical shapes for concepts reused across resource types (the $defs source for `common-elements.md` §2). A type references a shared shape via `$ref: common-elements.schema.json#/$defs/<Name>` rather than reinventing it — this is what makes the single-source rule enforceable for value shapes (SPEC-DESIGN §33). Prose definitions + the normalization plan live in `registry/common-elements.md`; this file is the normative machine mirror.

Quantity.description: A magnitude + explicit unit as one string (Kubernetes resource.Quantity form): decimal SI (K/M/G/T/P/E), binary (Ki/Mi/Gi/Ti/Pi/Ei), optional trailing B (MB/GiB), or milli (m). Never a bare number whose unit is implied by the field name. Use for memory, storage, bandwidth, power ("650W" — unit carried by the field), etc. Examples: "32GB", "1.5Gi", "500m", "200GB", "100PB".

ComputeResources.description: Canonical compute sizing. Reused by anything that sizes compute (VM, cluster node pool, database instance). `vcpu`/`cores`/`memory_gib` are non-canonical synonyms — normalize to cpu.count + memory.size.

instance_size.description: Provider-neutral, ordinally-comparable size class (e.g. small|medium|large|xlarge) — a portable "roughly this big", in place of or alongside explicit cpu/memory. UDLM owns the comparable vocabulary; the provider maps it to concrete cores/RAM at naturalization (DCM ADR-023).

StorageCapacity.description: A sized storage allocation. `size` is a Quantity; `storage_class` names the provider tier.

StorageUsage.$comment: common-elements.md §2.3 (usage detail — for pools/datasets/clusters that report utilization)
StorageUsage.description: Capacity + utilization, all Quantity. Used where a storage type reports allocated/free/usable (pool/dataset/cluster) rather than just a requested size.

cidr.description: A CIDR block — IPv4 ("192.0.2.0/24") or IPv6 ("2001:db8::/32"). Structural octet+prefix-length validation, replacing the weak per-type `^[0-9./]+$`.

ip_address.description: A single IP address — IPv4 or IPv6 (typically an outputs value).

Reference.description: A typed cross-entity pointer to another RESOURCE — distinct from a data_reference (ADR-012), which points at immutable reference-data. Authored by target_handle (AEP-124 resource association — reference by resource path); DCM resolves and pins target_uuid at reserve (ADR-025). The only cross-type binding surface besides typed outputs (E2). Never a provider-native id.

target_handle.description: AUTHORING KEY. The stable handle of the target resource (e.g. tenant-alpha-prod) — what an author or policy writes. A uuid is never hand-authored for a resource reference (ADR-025 §2).

target_uuid.description: RESOLUTION PROVENANCE — system-populated. DCM resolves the handle to this uuid at reserve and freezes it for deterministic replay, change-impact, and cross-boundary identity (ADR-025 §5). Guaranteed present only from the Realized state onward; when present it is honesty-gated + integrity-checked, a deferred reference may omit it until it resolves.

resolution_mode.description: resolved = target_uuid pinned now (the target exists); deferred = resolved by handle at reserve, tolerating a not-yet-existing target (claim-before-define — holds the resource in Requested until it resolves). Where deferred is disallowed is Policy (ADR-025 §6).

Condition.description: A status condition. `status[].conditions[]` is the canonical realized/discovered signal across all types.

Timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. Standard realized fields: create_time, update_time, last_transition_time.
